issues.title: Hook files placed directly in application/ layer instead of application/hooks/ subfolder
issues.description: 10 hook files are placed directly at the application/ layer root instead of inside a hooks/ subfolder. While the file names correctly use the 'use' prefix, they are inconsistent with the majority of hooks (39) which are organized under application/hooks/. This reduces discoverability and structural consistency.
issues.rule: Hooks should be placed in application/hooks/ subfolder for consistency
issues.fix: Move these files into application/hooks/ subfolders matching the pattern used by the majority of hooks (e.g., apps/store/src/features/cart/application/hooks/useFlyToCart.ts)

patterns.description: All React component files (.tsx) in components/ and pages/ folders correctly use PascalCase naming (e.g., AuditFilters.tsx, LoginPage.tsx).
All hook files correctly use camelCase with 'use' prefix (useAuth.ts, useProducts.ts, etc.).
All utility files correctly use camelCase (computeRole.ts, formatLastSeen.ts, getActionClass.ts, etc.).
All type files use either the descriptive PascalCase format (CategoryTypes.ts, GalleryTypes.ts) or domain-level types.ts filename.
Props interfaces consistently follow the ComponentNameProps pattern across all apps.
Internal state interfaces (RoleCard, ProviderConfig, SubmissionState, etc.) do not follow the Props suffix — correctly they are internal data shape types, not component prop interfaces.
Next.js special files (global-error.tsx, not-found.tsx) correctly use kebab-case as required by the framework, not a naming convention violation.
No kebab-case .tsx component files found outside the ui/ folder.
